item.title: GovBrief Today #453
item.description: Tim Kaine tried today to stop a war before it started, and 51 senators said he was too early because there was no war with Cuba. We have been in an undeclared war in Iran for sixty-two days. Thirteen American servicemembers are dead, gas is at $4.17, and U.S. bases in the region have sustained $5 billion in damage. The Senate had already voted down every war powers resolution on Venezuela and Iran. Today it added Cuba. Three Republicans broke ranks to even allow the debate. The other 50 told Trump he is free to do whatever he wants to Cuba. Call through 5 Calls and Resistbot and demand your senators stop the next war before it starts. I've emailed 7,176 stories over 453 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your name at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Easy-click links, data, images on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260428-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/charles-speech [2] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-merz [3] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-apr28 [4] https://go.govbrief.today/sc-voter-rolls [5] https://go.govbrief.today/az-ballot-win [6] https://go.govbrief.today/fcc-disney [7] https://go.govbrief.today/gas-high [8] https://go.govbrief.today/ship-searched [9] https://go.govbrief.today/ballroom-filing [10] https://go.govbrief.today/minn-warrants [11] https://go.govbrief.today/comey-seashell [12] https://go.govbrief.today/niaid-aide-conceal [13] https://go.govbrief.today/portland-tear-gas [14] https://go.govbrief.today/hurley-sidelined [15] https://go.govbrief.today/webster-retiring [16] https://go.govbrief.today/cuba-resolution [17] https://go.govbrief.today/epa-protesters [18] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-passport [19] https://go.govbrief.today/ice-detention-ruling Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: For all those who say Ukraine should just give land away. There are people on this land. And this is what happens to them: On this day in 2025..
item.description: .. Ukrainian authorities and media outlets publicly detailed the horrific condition of the body of Viktoriia Roshchyna, a 27-year-old investigative journalist who had disappeared in Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhia in August 2023 while reporting on civilians held by Russian forces. Her remains, repatriated to Ukraine as part of a February 2025 body exchange involving 757 fallen defenders, were initially labeled as an "unidentified male" and required DNA testing for confirmation. Forensic examinations revealed extensive signs of torture, including abrasions, hemorrhages, a broken rib, neck injuries, and possible electric shock burns on her feet. Russian officials had previously notified her family in October 2024 that she died on 19 September 2024, attributing it vaguely to heart failure. Roshchyna's case stands as a grim symbol of the risks faced by journalists documenting Russian aggression against Ukraine. This act of returning a desecrated corpse, alongside the documented torture, has been condemned as a Russian war crime. Read more about it in Ukraine Decoded, the Substack publication at https://ukrainedecoded.substack.com/p/russia-quietly-returns-mutilated
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.title: Prince solo guitar is honestly one of the best pieces I have ever seen.
item.description: I have had the greatest opportunity to see Prince twice. Not fully understanding his guitar prowess the first time, I was floored when he left the stage at The Gorge the second time, playing a 15 min solo through crowds. Couldn't see him, but the tidal swell around him, and the massive response was hard to miss. Fucking Legend.
item.dc:creator: ChicanoBatman
